title: Temporal prediction shifting for scalable video coding
abstract: A method (400) includes receiving an input video stream (120) and scaling the input video stream into two or more spatial layers (L). For each spatial layer, the method also includes generating a temporal layer prediction pattern (302) by: obtaining a temporal base layer (TL 0 ) for a corresponding spatial layer; identifying, based on the temporal base layer, a plurality of temporal layers (TL) and a plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during a temporal period (310); and aligning the temporal base layer for the corresponding spatial layer with one of the temporal time slots during the temporal period. Each temporal time slot is associated with one of the temporal base layer or one of the plurality of temporal layers for the corresponding spatial layer. The temporal base layer for each corresponding spatial layer is aligned with a different temporal time slot than each other temporal base layer.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method (400) comprising: receiving, at data processing hardware (144) of an encoder (200), an input video stream (120); scaling, by the data processing hardware (144), the input video stream (120) into two or more spatial layers (L); and for each spatial layer (L), generating, by the data processing hardware (144), a temporal layer prediction pattern (302) by: obtaining a temporal base layer (TLo) for a corresponding spatial layer (L); identifying, based on the temporal base layer (TLo), a plurality of temporal layers (TL) and a plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during a temporal period (310), each temporal time slot (312, 314, 316, 318) associated with one of the temporal base layer (TLo) or one of the plurality of temporal layers (TL) for the corresponding spatial layer (L); and aligning the temporal base layer (TLo) for the corresponding spatial layer (L) with one of the temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310), wherein the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) is aligned with a different temporal time slot (312, 314, 316, 318) than each other temporal base layer (TLo) for each other corresponding spatial layer (L). 2. The method (400) of claim 1, wherein obtaining the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) comprises: receiving a key frame (K) for a first spatial layer (L), the key frame (K) corresponding to the temporal base layer (TLo) for the first spatial layer (L); and predicting the temporal base layer (TLo) for a second spatial layer (L) based on the key frame (K). 3. The method (400) of claim 2, wherein predicting the temporal base layer (TLo) for the second spatial layer (L) comprises upsampling the key frame (K) for the first spatial 

 layer (L), the upsampled key frame (K) forming a reference frame for the second spatial layer (L) with greater resolution than the first spatial layer (L). 4. The method (400) of any of claims 1-3, wherein obtaining the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) comprises receiving a first key frame (K) as the temporal base layer (TLo) for a first spatial layer (L) and a second key frame (K) as the temporal base layer (TLo) for a second spatial layer (L). 5. The method (400) of any of claims 1-4, wherein a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310) is equal to where i corresponds to a number of the temporal layers (TL). 6. The method (400) of any of claims 1-5, wherein a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310) is independent of a number of the temporal layers (TL). 7. The method (400) of any of claims 1-6, wherein, at each spatial layer (L), the temporal period (310) comprises a plurality of hierarchical temporal layers (TL), the plurality of hierarchical temporal layers (TL) comprising at least one temporal layer (TL) predicted from a non-base temporal layer (TL). 8. The method (400) of claim 7, wherein the hierarchical temporal layers (TL) of the temporal period (310) are configured to provide a scalable frame rate for a bit stream (204) comprising the temporal layer prediction pattern (302). 9 The method (400) of any of claims 1-8, wherein a number of spatial layers (L) scaled from the input video stream (120) is independent from a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310). 10. The method (400) of any of claims 1-3, wherein aligning the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) distributes a bit rate allocated across all temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310). 11. An encoder (200) comprising: data processing hardware (144); and memory hardware (146) in communication with the data processing hardware (144), the memory hardware (146) storing instructions that when executed on the data processing hardware (144) cause the data processing hardware (144) to perform operations comprising: receiving an input video stream (120); scaling the input video stream (120) into two or more spatial layers (L); and for each spatial layer (L), generating a temporal layer prediction pattern (302) by: obtaining a temporal base layer (TLo) for a corresponding spatial layer (L); identifying, based on the temporal base layer (TLo), a plurality of temporal layers (TL) and a plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during a temporal period (310), each temporal time slot (312, 314, 316, 318) associated with one of the temporal base layer (TLo) or one of the plurality of temporal layers (TL) for the corresponding spatial layer (L); and aligning the temporal base layer (TLo) for the corresponding spatial layer (L) with one of the temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310), wherein the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) is aligned with a different temporal time slot (312, 314, 316, 318) than each other temporal base layer (TLo) for each other corresponding spatial layer (L). 12. The encoder (200) of claim 11, wherein obtaining the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) comprises: 

 receiving a key frame (K) for a first spatial layer (L), the key frame (K) corresponding to the temporal base layer (TLo) for the first spatial layer (L); and predicting the temporal base layer (TLo) for a second spatial layer (L) based on the key frame (K). 13. The encoder (200) of claim 12, wherein predicting the temporal base layer (TLo) for the second spatial layer (L) comprises upsampling the key frame (K) for the first spatial layer (L), the upsampled key frame (K) forming a reference frame for the second spatial layer (L) with greater resolution than the first spatial layer (L). 14. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-13, wherein obtaining the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) comprises receiving a first key frame (K) as the temporal base layer (TLo) for a first spatial layer (L) and a second key frame (K) as the temporal base layer (TLo) for a second spatial layer (L). 15. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-14, wherein a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310) is equal to 2 (t- L where i corresponds to a number of the temporal layers (TL). 16. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-15, wherein a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310) is independent of a number of the temporal layers (TL). 17. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-16, wherein, at each spatial layer (L), the temporal period (310) comprises a plurality of hierarchical temporal layers (TL), the plurality of hierarchical temporal layers (TL) comprising at least one temporal layer (TL) predicted from a non-base temporal layer (TL). 18. The encoder (200) of claim 17, wherein the hierarchical temporal layers (TL) of the temporal period (310) are configured to provide a scalable frame rate for a bit stream (204) comprising the temporal layer prediction pattern (302). 19. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-18, wherein a number of spatial layers (L) scaled from the input video stream (120) is independent from a number of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) for the plurality of temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310). 20. The encoder (200) of any of claims 11-19, wherein aligning the temporal base layer (TLo) for each corresponding spatial layer (L) distributes a bit rate allocated across all temporal time slots (312, 314, 316, 318) during the temporal period (310).
